query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Un lugar espectacular y, sin duda, una visita obligada para cualquiera que viaje a Manuel Antonio.

El parque está muy bien mantenido, los senderos principales son cómodos de recorrer y todo se encuentra en excelente estado. Nosotros realizamos únicamente el recorrido principal, ya que no tuvimos tiempo de explorar todos los senderos secundarios, pero la experiencia fue muy buena de principio a fin.

Las playas son, sencillamente, impresionantes. De hecho, creemos que una de ellas podría estar fácilmente entre las mejores playas de Costa Rica. El agua es tranquila, el entorno es hermoso y la combinación entre selva y playa crea un paisaje realmente único.

Nuestro principal consejo es llegar lo más temprano posible. Si lográs adelantarte a los grupos organizados, podés disfrutar varias horas con muy poca gente y vivir una experiencia mucho más tranquila. Nosotros estuvimos en temporada baja y durante la mañana la playa se disfrutaba muchísimo. Sin embargo, hacia el mediodía comenzaron a llegar más grupos y la cantidad de visitantes aumentó considerablemente. Imaginamos que durante la temporada alta esto debe ser aún más notorio.

Algo que sí consideramos importante mencionar ocurre antes de ingresar al parque. En los accesos hay varias personas que se acercan a los visitantes para ofrecer estacionamiento, guías o distintos servicios. Algunas incluso pueden dar la impresión de estar vinculadas al parque, lo que puede generar confusión para quienes visitan por primera vez.

Nuestra recomendación es informarse previamente sobre los servicios oficiales, no detenerse apresuradamente ante la primera persona que los llame y comparar opciones antes de decidir dónde estacionar o contratar una excursión. Nosotros tuvimos una experiencia incómoda intentando aclarar precios y condiciones, por lo que vale la pena tomarse unos minutos para verificar la información antes de aceptar cualquier servicio.

Más allá de eso, la experiencia dentro del parque fue excelente. Naturaleza espectacular, playas increíbles y senderos muy bien cuidados. Un lugar que definitivamente volveríamos a visitar.

Nous avons réservé une visite avec guide privé et la prise en charge à l'hôtel directement via le Parc national Manuel Antonio.
La visite avec le guide et la découverte du parc se sont bien passées même si la visite se fait un peu au pas de course.
Je suis cependant vraiment déçue par le service. Après la visite nous avons repris le bus qui nous avait déposé au parc et lorsque nous sommes arrivés à l’hôtel, le chauffeur nous a réclamé 5 $ par personne, soit un total de 25 $ en nous disant que juste l'aller était inclus.
À aucun moment le prestataire ni le chauffeur ne nous ont dit que le trajet de retour serait facturé en supplément ni n’ont mentionné le prix.
Deux Uber nous auraient coûté deux fois moins cher.
Soyez prudents, c'est une vraie arnaque !

Warning for other tourists visiting Manuel Antonio National Park.

Before reaching the official entrance, we were stopped by unofficial “parking attendants” wearing fake official-looking gear and pressured into paying $20 cash for parking. They also aggressively tried to force us to hire a guide.

When I asked for a receipt, they could not provide one and just shrugged. The whole situation felt misleading and dishonest, especially because they acted as if they were part of the national park system.

Do not stop for random people on the road. Drive directly to the official entrance and be careful with anyone claiming guides or parking are mandatory.

Better to go to any other National Park!!!

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Der Nationalpark Manuel Antonio in Costa Rica gehört zu den bekanntesten Nationalparks des Landes und ist vor allem dafür bekannt, dass er auf relativ kleiner Fläche sehr viel Abwechslung bietet. Besonders ist die Kombination aus dichtem Regenwald, tierreicher Natur und schönen Stränden, die direkt ineinander übergehen und sich gut an einem Tag erleben lassen.

Die Wege im Park sind gut ausgebaut und auch für normale Besucher ohne große Wandererfahrung gut machbar. Unterwegs kann man mit etwas Glück viele Tiere sehen, zum Beispiel Affen, Faultiere, Leguane und verschiedene Vogelarten. Genau diese Tierbeobachtungen machen den Park für viele so interessant.

Ein Highlight sind die Strände im Park. Sie sind sehr schön, mit hellem Sand und türkisfarbenem Wasser, und liegen direkt am Dschungel. Dadurch wirkt alles sehr ursprünglich und deutlich besonderer als viele klassische Badeorte.

Allerdings ist der Park auch außerhalb der Hauptsaison gut besucht. Selbst im Juni, also eigentlich in der Nebensaison, war es bei unserem Besuch schon ziemlich voll, sowohl auf den Wegen als auch an den Stränden. Das nimmt ein bisschen von der Ruhe, die man in einem Nationalpark eigentlich erwarten würde. Außerdem muss man Tickets meist im Voraus buchen, da der Eintritt begrenzt ist.

Insgesamt ist Manuel Antonio trotzdem ein sehr lohnenswertes Reiseziel, besonders wenn man zum ersten Mal in Costa Rica ist und Natur, Tiere und Strand in kurzer Zeit erleben möchte. Wer es allerdings ruhiger und weniger touristisch mag, sollte eher andere, weniger bekannte Parks in Betracht ziehen.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
This park was worth every bit of the effort made to get to it. Some things to add that may be helpful when planning your trip: Buy tickets in advance on the SINAC website and book for the earliest time possible (I did 7am). You cannot purchase at the location. There are bathrooms and showers throughout the park near the beaches. There’s a restaurant with a decent variety of foods, juices, ice cream, etc. which is wonderful since absolutely no food of any sort can be brought inside the park, not even chewing gum. Your bags will be searched upon entering. You don’t HAVE to have a guide to see wildlife, but if seeing sloths are the top of the list, you may consider using one. I just watched other groups with a guide and looked at where they were watching and was able to see one (usually up high in trees). Monkeys were in multiple areas, and I got to see them up close. If you do all the trails, it’s a little over 5 miles. I did them all in one day, and I’d recommend wearing good shoes and not just slide on shoes.

PARKING: People will be flagging and whistling your car down multiple times before you can even see the entrance of the park. Do not stop! Keep going until you can see the entrance, and there will be a well trusted parking area on the LEFT right before the entrance. They’re called Chalo Parking, and it costs 5,000 colones ($10USD) to park here. They also may need to take your key and do valet parking because it gets cramped in there. IT IS OKAY TO TRUST THEM. To ensure a parking spot at the closest parking area, get to the park between 7:00-7:30am.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
I absolutely LOVED this place and if you are a nature and animal lover you will too!

We got the earliest possible reservation and it was worth it as we had a few hours of peace and quiet before the crowds started to come in. However, they only let a certain amount of people in at a time to keep crowds under control. NOTE: You MUST make your time slot or risk not being let in.

Once in you literally step into a prestine jungle with stunning protected beaches. Wildlife is everywhere, but it is very hot and humid so on the day I went the animals weren't that active.

Make sure you know where to park and decide if you want a tour guide because as soon as you pull up you will have locals hustling you for both. They weren't rude or aggressive, you just have to be firm with them.

Be prepared to WALK and make sure to have plenty if water in a reusable container as they don't allow outside food or beverages (for beverages, specially no plastic bottles). The trailers aren't that bad, but there are a ton of stairs so take your time.

There are stunning vistas everywhere so the trailers and stairs are worth it. PRO TIP: Bring binoculars if you want to get the clearest views of animals. Also, if you really love nature and animals it might be worth a two day tip as the park closes at 3PM for entrance and everyone has to leave by 4PM local time. So, I'd suggest explore and enjoy the beaches and trails day one and scout of the best pictures and animal habitats. Then on day 2 bring all your equipment for those professionals shots where you can take your time and not be rushed.

Lastly, the beaches are awesome but the tide comes in quick so be sure to go early and enjoy the beaches first and hike afterwards if you planned to enjoy the beaches.

Pura vida!!

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica

review_text:
Manuel Antonio National Park es una auténtica pasada. Súper accesible y fácil de recorrer incluso. La combinación entre selva tropical y playas espectaculares hace que el paisaje tenga un contraste increíble.

Además, el parque está muy bien cuidado, es amigable para visitar y en unas horas puedes ver naturaleza, fauna y algunas de las playas más bonitas que he visto. Totalmente recomendado.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
As other reviews mentioned, we got pulled over by guys in uniform pretending to be work workers. They had a stop point before you get to the beach. Tried to charge us $150 for two people, saying you can’t buy the park tickets for today, you have to buy a day in advance and you can park only here, to the right for the park and to the left for the beach for a fee. They were extremely rude. I ended up driving away. Then after passing the beach, several more guys were flagging us down, each trying to get us to park in their spots. I was in a bad mood before even getting to the park. They were threatening with police saying that if we don’t park where they say, the traffic police might fine us and take away our license plates. We ended up parking in the big parking lot right before the park entrance and paid 4000 colones ($9 US).

At the park entrance and throughout, we were getting harassed by the tour guides telling us to pay them and not go by ourselves.

(Be aware, you cannot bring plastic bottles into the park or any food, they will make you throw it away. Totallly ok, just something to be aware of. We had metal bottles so it was no problem.)

Inside the park, we did see some monkeys and crabs, on one of the paths upcoming tour guide with a group warned us there’s a viper snake on the side and not to go there as it is deadly if bites so we turned around. Grateful for the warning!

Park itself was ok, we were coming from La Fortuna and I’ve seen many national parks before, so this was underwhelming. Too many people (even on Monday), felt very commercialized.

I wouldn’t come here for the park, but the beach at the end was gorgeous! If anything, it’s worth coming here if you don’t mind dealing with swarms of scammers.

Am Rande dieser Strasse befinden sich die Tiere. Jeder Guide erkämpft sich seinen Platz für seine Gruppe. Eine gestresste Zeit und ein gestresster Guide. Schade, eigentlich wäre der Guide sehr kompetent gewesen und er hat uns mit dem Natel schöne Foto ab dem Teleskop gemacht.
Im Restaurant kauften wir ein teures Sandwich das so trocken war wie die Sahara!

It is highly recommended to hire a guide if you want to spot the different animals there, because they hide very well. Use only an official or certified guide (they can be spotted by the telescope and the badge). Don't miss it!

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: El parque en si es un sueño, la naturaleza por si sola es un 5 estrellas. El puntaje de 3 estrellas se lo doy a las personas básicamente. En nuestra llegada 8am se nos tiraban encima para que estacionamos el auto (personas con identificación) y se lo dejamos a un muchacho que nos trató muy mal. Luego en la entrada te sacan comida y bebidas, la comida se entiende porque no hay que alimentar a los animales pero asi y todo adentro hay un restaurante entonces no se conduce con lo de la entrada, claramente es un negocio. Luego a diferencia de cahuita por ejemplo, hay mucha mas gente y tuvimos un guía de un grupo que andaba por ahí que palmeo dos veces a mi novia para hacerla apurar porque habia un perezoso y realmente fue muy maltratador. Todo esto que les cuento hace que mi puntaje no sea 5, la playa y los animales son y serán siempre un 1000 estrellas ♡

review_text:
Honestly there are other places to see wildlife than here! The park is overcrowded with tourists and you don't see much wildlife except insects and monkeys.

You can't bring plastic water bottles or ANY food! Eat before going because the food inside the park canteen is absolutely horrible. There is a line to go pay for the food, another line to pickup the food, another line to pick up the coffee! What a waste of time and after all this the food is bland and inedible!!

The beaches are beautiful but too rough to swim in plus there are jellyfish in the water. When I was there no one was swimming due to the storng currents that pull you in. There are more beautiful beaches in the world. I felt so disappointed by this trip overall.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: Cool park and definitely amazing beaches with some that are more secluded for a more private lounge. However it is extremely crowded, and there are huge guided tour groups that block the walking passage, especially later on in the morning, so I would recommend to come in as early as possible. There is also little shade coverage so at around 12/1 pm it is straight sunshine- perfect for tanning but the UV is strong so can cause sunburn if you are not protected correctly. No food or plastic water bottles allowed. They have storage lockers but you have to pay for them ($5). Tickets need to be purchased in advance online.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Did a guided walking tour and thoroughly enjoyed my time at the park. At the end of the tour, you're dropped off at the beach and can enjoy a beach day. I recommend that you eat a good meal before the tour and bring water because you'll do a lot of walking, as the tour is about 2 hours. In addition, you've got to buy a ticket to the park through the government as most tours don't include your ticket to the park. You can risk buying "day of" but they may run out of passes for the hour you're visiting.

As you enter the park people will begin to ask you about parking, I recommend just saying you have a ticket and you have a parking lot close to the park so you don't have to walk long.

review_text:
Wow!!!! What an incredible experience.
We booked through Viator, entry plus guide was 39£ per person. During the two hour tour we saw a handful of different animals including two snakes, many lizards, iguanas, many birds, five different sloths, coatis, deers, bats, white faced monkeys. It was really amazing!!!!
Once the tour was finished, we walked around the park again two more times ( other guides kindly showed us through their telescopes the animals )
We then spent time on the beaches, paddling and cooling down in the water.
Overall you can easily spend from opening to
Close time wondering around and taking in the beautiful landscape and animals.
10/10 can’t recommend enough ⭐️

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Un sendero central y otros alternativos te llevan a conocer flora y fauna. Por momentos estas en el medio de la selva, que huele a tierra mojada y se oye el mar. Los monos andan por todos lados, es importante no alimentarlos y tampoco asustarlos. Solo dejar que ellos caminen con vos o anden libres por su hábitat.
Hay cangrejos, arañas, víboras, iguanas. Y hay tambien perezosos!
Al final del recorrido esta la playa para relajar y descansar. Ojo con las pertenencias porque los monos andan por ahi y querrán ver que tienes! Son curiosos.
Se puede ir de ojotas, pero recomiendo un calzado cómodo como zapatillas. Agua solo en botellas de metal. No se permite ingresar con comida o botellas de plástico.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Visitamos el Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio y, aunque es un lugar muy conocido, nuestra experiencia no fue del todo positiva.

Es importante saber que las entradas deben comprarse con bastante antelación, especialmente en época de vacaciones. De lo contrario, prácticamente la única opción es contratar un tour con agencia, lo que encarece bastante la visita.

Al llegar a la entrada, llama la atención la cantidad de negocio alrededor del parque: personas en la calle indicando (e insistiendo) dónde aparcar, ya que todos los aparcamientos son de pago. Esto genera una sensación algo incómoda desde el principio.

Dentro del parque, aunque el entorno es bonito, no tuvimos la suerte de ver muchos animales. Además, parte del recorrido transcurre por caminos de grava que no transmiten la sensación de estar en un entorno tan natural como en otros parques del país.

En conjunto, la experiencia tuvo más aspectos negativos que positivos. Una pena, porque esperábamos mucho más de este parque.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Manuel Antonio National Park is a great place to visit. It can get a bit too crowded, but that’s because it’s such a beautiful and popular park.

It has a perfect balance of being wild yet very easy to walk around, with well-maintained paths. The beaches inside the park are stunning, and there’s plenty of wildlife—you can see a lot of animals along the way. I explored it on my own without a guide and still saw a lot of animals, which was amazing.

Overall, a really great park and definitely worth visiting.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica

review_text:
Le parc est magnifique on y croise beaucoup d'espèces mais avec l'aide d'un guide. Prévoyez à l'avance avec des sociétés comme World évasion.

⚠️Attention lorsque que vous vous rendez au parc
⚠️Attention arnaque parking - Parc Manuel Antonio

À quelques centaines de mètres avant l'entrée du parc, des personnes en gilet vous font signe de vous arrêter en disant que le parking est complet.
🚨Ce ne sont PAS des employés officiels.
Leur but est de vous faire vous garer plus loin et vous faire payer plus cher.
Le vrai parking est juste à côté de l'entrée et coûte environ 5000 colones.
Conseil : ne vous arrêtez pas et continuez votre route.
Si on insiste, dites simplement que vous allez dans un hôtel à côté de l'entrée (comme l’hôtel Espadilla ou l’hôtel Villa Bosque), ils vous laisseront passer.

review_text:
Unfortunately this beautiful place has been ruined by the people that work there.  Online ticket purchase takes so much of your personal information - email, home address, phone number, AND the passport numbers of each guest.  And of course your credit card.

From the moment you approach you'll be aggressively flagged by people jumping in front of your car trying to get you to park in their "secure" parking spot for a fee.

Walking through the intense security you are then harassed by "tour guides" that call you stupid for not wanting them and warn you that you're not going to see anything and you'll regret it.

Once inside the park, don't dare to look in the same direction as a guides telescope because they will again harass you for being cheap and not paying, ridiculing you in front of their group saying things like "see how these people follow us and don't pay".  We were walking on the same path... it's a park.  And they were looking at just an iguana... it's an iguana guy.

It's really a shame, if the people weren't so aggressively annoying I would definitely recommend this beautiful place.  If you're looking for calm nature, stay away.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
El 6 de abril visitamos el Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio con un grupo de 30 personas.

Lo primero que debo decir es que, cuando viajas con un grupo grande, la compra de entradas se vuelve bastante complicada. Sería mucho más práctico contar con opciones específicas para grupos, no necesariamente en taquilla, pero sí algún sistema que facilite este proceso.

En nuestro caso, llegamos aproximadamente 2 horas después de nuestra hora de entrada, y curiosamente esto jugó a nuestro favor, ya que había menos gente en los senderos. Gracias a eso pudimos disfrutar mucho más la experiencia y observar varias especies: monos capuchinos, una familia de monos tití a muy corta distancia, dos perezosos, serpientes, basiliscos, entre otros.

La única desventaja fue que ya no tuvimos mucho tiempo para disfrutar la playa, ya que el parque cierra relativamente temprano. Sin embargo, también es importante entender que estamos en un área protegida: nosotros somos los visitantes y es fundamental respetar los tiempos y el entorno de los animales.
En cuanto a las instalaciones, los baños actualmente están en malas condiciones, aunque se nota que están en proceso de mejora, lo cual es una excelente noticia.
Sobre los alimentos, hay un cambio muy positivo. Hace un año se permitía el ingreso con comida y eso generaba mucho desorden, sobre todo por la interacción con los monos. En esta ocasión, se percibe un mejor control, menos contacto con la fauna y un parque mucho más limpio.

Sin duda, es un lugar al que volveríamos. Solo dejaría como recomendación mejorar la agilidad en el proceso de acceso para grupos.

name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Visiting Manuel Antonio National Park is truly an unforgettable experience. This park offers a perfect combination of lush rainforest, abundant wildlife, and some of the most beautiful beaches in Costa Rica.
From the moment you enter, you are surrounded by nature. It is common to see monkeys, sloths, iguanas, and a variety of birds along the trails, so it is worth taking your time and looking closely. The walking paths are well maintained and accessible, making it enjoyable for visitors of all ages.
The beaches inside the park are absolutely stunning, with soft white sand and clear, calm water that is perfect for swimming. The contrast between the dense green jungle and the bright blue ocean creates breathtaking views.
Be prepared for crowds, especially during peak hours, and it is highly recommended to arrive early. Also, bring water, sunscreen, and patience, as the experience is worth it.
Overall, Manuel Antonio National Park is a must-visit destination for anyone traveling to Costa Rica. It offers a unique blend of adventure, relaxation, and natural beauty that leaves a lasting impression.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Wir haben den Nationalpark in der absoluten Hochsaison besucht – an unseren Besuchstag gab es keine Karten mehr zu kaufen!
Unsere Tipps für den Besuch:
1.) Am wichtigsten ist es sehr früh zu kommen. Am besten ist es schon kurz vor sieben am Eingang zu sein, da der Park bereit um 7:00 Uhr öffnet. die Tickets muss man vorab im Internet kaufen.
2.) Am besten mal kommt mit dem Bus oder Taxi, da der Parkplatz bereits um 7:30 Uhr komplette überfüllt ist und zudem 10$ Parkgebühr kostet. Das Taxi kostete von unserer Unterkunft nur fünf Dollar.
3.) Man braucht nicht unbedingt einen Guide für den Park, denn die meisten Tiere kann man auch selbst beobachten beziehungsweise wenn irgendwo ein Tier sichtbar ist bildet sich sowieso eine Traube von Menschen. Humor muss sich einfach nur dazu stellen, um das Tier zu beobachten.
4.) Der Park ist ziemlich klein und daher bilden sich schon in der Früh nach dem Eingangsbereich große Menschenansammlungen. Wir sind daher relativ schnell Richtung Punta Catedral gegangen und waren sowohl am Playa als auch auf dem Rundweg am Punta Catedral so gut wie alleine!
Zusammenfassend lässt sich sagen, dass der Nationalpark Sehr klein ist, jedoch bei frühen Besuch durchaus individuell begangen werden kann. Es gibt sehr viele Tiere in dem Nationalpark, die man am besten in der Früh sehen kann. Sobald sich der Park mit Menschen füllt, beziehungsweise es zur Mittagszeit heißer wird, ziehen sich die meisten Tiere ins Innere in Schattenplätze zurück!

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Ich war vor 11 Jahren das erste mal im Nationalpark und war begeistern. Diesmal war ich enttäuscht.

Für den Nationalpark muss man vorab ein Ticket online kaufen. Wir haben 5 Tage im voraus buchen müssen auf SINAC, weil alles andere ausverkauft war. Ein Ticket kostet 16 Dollar.

Wir sind mit dem Auto hingefahren und wollten ohne Guide entspannt durch den Park laufen.

Vor dem Park ist ein Parkplatz bei dem man 5000 Colones oder 10 Dollar Cash zahlt. Auf dem Weg dort hin wurden wir angehalten und uns wurde gesagt, dass wir ohne Guide nur zum Strand können und nicht den Park und Tiere sehen dürften. Ich hatte vorher bereits gelesen, dass so einige Guides versuchen Besucher zu einem Guide zu überreden.

Plastik Trinkflaschen und Essen sind aus Naturschutzgründen verboten. Man kann feste Wasserflaschen mitnehmen. In der Mitte des Parks gibt’s ein Restaurant. Das Essen sah schlecht aus und Getränke sind teuer. Unverständlich war, warum man Cola in einem Pappbecher bekommt aber Wasser nur in einer festen Flasche für 9 Dollar. Die Flasche ist schön gemacht und kann ein Souvenir sein. Wir wollten nur Wasser trinken, haben es aber nicht eingesehen 9 Dollar zu zahlen.

Der Park war überfüllt mit Menschen. Wir haben in der freien Natur Costa Ricas und anderen Parks bereits fast alle Tiere gesehen, die wir auch dort gesehen haben. Die Atmosphäre an anderen Orten war viel schöner.

Der Strand hat uns an einige Strände auf Mallorca erinnert. Wer die Insel kennt, weiß was ich meine. Wir sind hier direkt wieder weg, nachdem wir Tiere im Park gesehen haben und sind zu einem unberührten Strand an der Pazifikküste gefahren (ohne Eintrittsgebühren).
Der Strand ist bestimmt ganz toll, wenn nicht so viele Menschen da wären.

Ich würde hier nicht nochmal hingehen.

name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Sin duda, es uno de los parques más hermosos de Costa Rica. La playa está impecable, el entorno es espectacular y se nota el gran esfuerzo por proteger y conservar la fauna que habita en el lugar.
El único aspecto a mejorar es el tema de los guías. Resulta curioso que, en un país tan ordenado, esta parte parezca poco regulada: desde personas que interceptan a los visitantes antes de llegar al estacionamiento, hasta el hecho de que, sin un guía, puede ser difícil apreciar bien a los animales, aunque estén a la vista de todos.
Mi recomendación sería establecer una regulación más clara, integrando el servicio de guías de forma oficial, con tarifas definidas y una oferta transparente que mejore la experiencia para todos los visitantes.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica

review_text: La visita a este Parque Nacional, fue una experiencia agradable, con senderos muy bien cuidados, en donde encontramos gran variedad de aves, oso hormiguero, perezoso, y como hay manglares hay cocodrilos, una playa muy linda, pero hay que caminar bastante, no permiten ingresar alimentos, pero hay dentro para comer, en donde las jaulas son para los visitantes evitando que los monos traviesos no te quiten tus alimento, muy vigilado.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Do tre stelle principalmente per il mare, che è davvero spettacolare e da solo vale la visita. Anche la vegetazione è molto bella, rigogliosa e ben conservata, ed è piacevole passeggiare nei sentieri immersi nella natura.
Purtroppo però l’esperienza complessiva non è all’altezza delle aspettative. La presenza di animali è stata quasi nulla, vedere un pappagallo o un tucano è praticamente impossibile, onestamente mi aspettavo molto di più da un parco di questo livello... visto come viene presentato..
Altro punto critico è l’organizzazione, già dall’ingresso si percepisce confusione e poca gestione dei flussi, con attese e poca chiarezza.
In sintesi, posto molto bello dal punto di vista paesaggistico, ma con una gestione migliorabile e un’esperienza naturalistica decisamente sotto le aspettative.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Ein recht preiswerter (ca. 5-6€) Nationalpark, welcher aber massiv überlaufen ist. Wir haben in etwa 100m Reichweite auf einem sehe großen Parkplatz geparkt und 5000 colones bezahlt.
Den Park haben wir mit einem Guide gebucht, welcher circa 50€ (2 stunden) gekostet hat.
Ohne den Guide wären uns diverse Tiere nicht aufgefallen, was also sehr hilfreich und informativ war.

Die erste Hälfte des Nationalparks ist relativ kurz, danach kommt ein wunderschöner Strand zum Entspannen. Wir haben während unserer Costa Eica Reise einige Strände besucht und sind der festen Überzeugung, dass der Strand im Nationalpark der schönste ist!

Die Mitnahme von Getränken in Plastikflaschen, sowie die Mitnahme von Speisen sind ausdrücklich verboten. Eine wiederbefüllbare Flasche aus Metall beispielsweise ist allerdings erlaubt.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: I am giving a one star to this place for the management, since we booked online as advised, since park entrance only was sold out we booked a guided tour which included parking, a guide, and a parking , 4 of us coasted us ~$400. Actual cost to park is ~$20, plus cost of tickets would be ~$80, which would be $100 for all us. The guide really didn’t care about the tour and just rushed to get to the beach as quickly as possible. Overcrowded with lots of tourists trying to the same thing, you really can’t enjoy the nature because everyone is there looking at the same one wild life. You don’t benefit a guide to walk ~2km, very well marked. If you show up on the day of and get the individual ticket and just decline every guild that approaches you and go straight to the beach.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Très belle expérience, nous sommes venus dès l’ouverture (7h) et avons pu profiter du calme avant les grosses vagues de touristes. Nous avons eu les plages pour nous tous seuls quasiment. Également vu beaucoup de singes sur la boucle au croisement des plages Espadilla et Manuel Antonio. On a profité de la plage Manuel Antonio pendant quelques heures. Après nous avons fait un crochet par le mirador (attention bcp de marches à la montée)
Dernier détour par la cascade proche de l’entrée, mais malheureusement à cette période de l’année il n’y a presque pas d’eau, donc sentier pour rien
Pour le parking, on recommande El Chalo (10$ pour la journée) juste à côté de l’entrée du parc. Ne vous faites pas avoir par le gros parking juste après le virage.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
I visited MA National Park in April 2026 & this park is must to do activities in Manual Antonio.

Park was crowded  for early morning season. This is absolutely fun park with lots of Animals, specially monkeys, Sloths, birds, snakes, lizards & frogs.
Young kids will have lots of fun.

Within the park, there multiple beaches & they were beautiful & clean water.
There are showers, restrooms & a food restaurant inside the park.

Don't bring plastic water bottles with you, park doesn't allow you to carry inside.
Also keep rain jacket/umbrella with you along with swimwear & towels. You won't be able to buy any swimwear inside the park, however there are many shops just outside the entrance.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: I don't really get why it's so popular, maybe we just got unlucky and there weren't that many animals, but beyond some far off sloths there wasn't really much to see. Sort of a nice walk through the trees on a large but extremely busy trail, huge your groups everywhere crowding around what little there is to see. The ticket system is super annoying have to book far in advance for a date and time, and definitely sells out, I think mostly to guides trying to sell overpriced tours. Beach area is beautiful, but so is the public beach that you don't need a ticket to go to. Unless we were just unlucky or there at the totally wrong time of year (early April) I have no idea what the big draw was. If you want a pleasant walk perhaps the regular ticket price worth it, I would definitely skip any expensive guides. Word of warning the check your bags at the entrance and you can not bring in disposable plastic water bottles.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Manuel Antonio National Park is often praised as one of Costa Rica’s most beautiful destinations, and the scenery truly lives up to the reputation. Unfortunately, visiting during high season comes with a major downside: overwhelming crowds. We arrived early in the morning, yet the park was already packed with large groups and countless guides leading small tours along the main trails. It felt congested from the very beginning, which made it hard to enjoy the experience fully.

The beach, usually a highlight of the park, was also completely overcrowded after the tours ended. Only on a few of the smaller side trails—those not used by the guides—did we find some peace and quiet. While the natural beauty is undeniable, the sheer number of visitors significantly limited the overall experience.

review_text:
The park it self is extremely beautiful and a must visit !!

It is warm/hot so bring a non disposable form of water like a yeti or something like that

Depending on your guide or whether you go alone can make a big difference, since the guides know what to look for in the trees

The guides do seemed rushed to get as many groups as they can so that can be sucky

If you don’t jump in the ocean (which the guides told us can be dangerous  because of the waves and riptides l) the trail could be walked in about 2 hours

Do not try to bring in any of the things that are posted not to like sunscreen snacks tobacco, they do check your bags and will make you throw it away or put it in a locker

Tons of sloths, monkeys, birds to be seen  !

When getting to the park beware of people trying to get you to park in their lots ! Some seemed sketchy and a lot of the people seemed quite pushy fighting over cars.

But that’s on the outside of the park

Kid friendly

Wheelchair friendly

One time kind of thing

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Nationalpark Manuel Antonio – absolute Enttäuschung.

Komplett überlaufen, man wird durchgeschleust wie in einem Freizeitpark. Von Naturerlebnis keine Spur. Kaum Tiere gesehen – und wenn, dann nur mit Glück und zwischen Menschenmassen.

Die Organisation ist schlecht, das Ticketsystem unnötig kompliziert und mühsam. Vor Ort fühlt sich niemand wirklich zuständig, die Mitarbeitenden wirken desinteressiert und unmotiviert.

Preis-Leistung stimmt überhaupt nicht. Viel zu teuer für das, was man bekommt.

Fazit: Spart euch den Aufwand. Einer der enttäuschendsten Orte in Costa Rica.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica

Hours of sitting in the sun, waiting for construction. Air conditioning off to prevent car overheating. It was not worth it, a trip spoiler.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Manuel Antonio is a must for anybody that is visiting Costa Rica for wildlife viewing. It is good to take a guided tour, because many of the guides share wildlife spottings with each other.
Also, if you don't hire a guide, you can go at your own pace for as long as you want (or until the park closes).
The beach inside the park is beautiful and you can take a dip to cool off from walking around the park. The park is closed on Tuesdays, but open every other day from 7:00am until 3:00pm. Pura Vida!

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Absolutely stunning national park — easily one of the most beautiful places I’ve visited. The scenery is incredible, with lush greenery, pristine beaches, and wildlife everywhere you look. We saw monkeys, birds, and even some animals we didn’t expect, which made the whole experience feel really special and immersive.

That said, one thing to be aware of: they are very strict about nicotine products. They will confiscate anything like that at the entrance, no exceptions. It caught me a bit off guard, so just be prepared and plan accordingly.

Overall, an amazing place that’s 100% worth visiting — just know the rules ahead of time so there are no surprises.

review_text:
Loved the beaches and National Park if we could have just paid without a guide. We were told the $18 tickets were not available for 3 days. If we wanted to go in we needed to book with a guide who would be with us and a group for 2 hours, at a cost os $133 for both of us. I would warn to book in advance. We were in the 0800 group along with masses of people queuing for over half an hour. 3000 people a day go in. The guide took an hour to go 100 yds. We were happy when the tour was over and spotted more wildlife left to ourselves.
Saying that the beaches were beautiful, we saw sloths as usual high in the trees
I agree that no food or plastic bottles should be allowed,bags were searched, as there was not any rubbish.
All this said if we had known and got the normal priced tickets it would have been even better. Others had better guides
Beware it's crowded at the start

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
This is a beautiful national park.
The jungle and beaches are amazing.
We hired a guide and would highly recommend that. You will have a hard time seeing a lot of the wildlife otherwise.
They know where to look and they use spotting scopes that are superior to trying to find things with binoculars on your own.
They're also very knowledgeable and provide for a fun experience.
But, you can definitely enjoy the park on your own as well. You just won't see some of the harder to spot wildlife without a guide.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: Rispetto ad altri parchi visitati ( tipo Tortuguero) questo  è stata una mezza delusione. Una folla all'ingresso e sui sentieri, sembrava di essere in coda per entrare alla Cappella Sistina. Gruppi che si incrociano, bambini che strillano, adulti che chiacchierano come fossero sulla passeggiata a mare di Sanremo. Animali avvistati pochi, un tour fatto a passo di carica, e arrivati alla spiaggia la nostra guida ci congeda indicandoci il sentiero per l'uscita. Ci gira le foto che ha fatto e tanti saluti. Poche le spiegazioni, solo la fretta di andare a recuperare l'infornata di turisti successivi. Per fortuna mentre eravamo sulla spiaggia per i fatti nostri sono arrivate numerose iguana e tante scimmie cappuccine. Non vale la pena venire fino qui, la Costa Rica ha migliori attrazioni.

review_text: Super beau parc.  Une belle plage côté pacifique! Plein d'animaux a voir.   Ne vous faites pas avoir avec les parkings. query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: Das Erlebnis war nicht so gut. Es gab totales Chaos auf den Straßen und mit den Parkplätzen, eng und hektisch, extrem viele Menschen. Die vermeintlichen Parkwächter werfen sich regelrecht vor die Autos, um sie zu stoppen. Wenn man nicht bei ihnen parkt, dann wird man beschimpft. Wir waren am Morgen dort, und der Park war extrem voll, sowas haben wir wirklich noch nicht gesehen. Wir hatten einen Guide, aber der hat auch nur da geschaut, wo alle anderen waren. Am Eingang sind sehr unfreundliche Menschen, die Taschenkontrollen machen. Man wird dort wie ein Häftling behandelt. Das Restaurant im Park ist ein absolutes No-Go, eingegittert, hier wieder das Gefühl, im Gefängnis zu sein. Der Strand ist wunderschön, aber auch sehr überfüllt. Das Erlebnis kann man sich sparen, es gibt weitaus schönere Spots in Costa Rica.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Einen Stern ganz klar für den Sauberen Strand und den gut begehbaren sauberen Park.

Und wieder mal eine überfüllte Touristenattraktion….
Zuerst braucht man viel Geduld beim Anstehen🥵 Endlich am Gate angekommen tummeln sich Gruppen von Gruppen mit Guide, und die Überfüllte Masse zieht los um endlich das lang ersehnte Faultier zu sehen. Endlich, da hing dann mal eines und die Masse an Menschen freute sich…
Ein paar kleine Leguane 2-3 Vögel und ein paar Affen aber das alles gibt es ja auch außerhalb in hülle und fülle….
2x 18.- Dollar weg und 10.- Dollar für Parken.
Fazit: Spart euch das Geld für den National-Park, wir haben so viele Tiere ausserhalb gesehen da braucht es keinen Park!

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Overcrowded park that doesn't live up to its reputation. You need to reserve your spot with large advance, you get searched  and they ban all food, so you've no choice but to eat absolutely crap (expensive too) from a caged area (yes, a big cage) in the middle of the park. The guides are not nice, entitled and oftentimes unqualified chancers. Plenty of other parks that offer a more relaxed, casual experience andbetter chances of spotting wildlife. I spent three months in Costa Rica and the best encounters were free.
On the plus side, very nice beach.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica

review_text: El lugar es muy ordenado y exigen al visitante cumplir con sus reglas, por ejemplo no llevar comida y que las botellas de agua no sean plásticas. Muy limpio y el paisaje es impresionante. Lleva binoculares para observar los animales y así no necesitarás guía.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: El parque combina naturaleza y playa, algo fantástico para mí!! Una caminata muy agradable con la posibilidad de ver perezosos y algún otro animal....monos se ven sin buscarlos cerca de playa Manuel Antonio. No poder ingresar nada de comyes algo extremo, habrán tenido incidentes o gente que alimenta a los monos....el tema del parking no me gusto nada, grupos que se cruzan en la calle para que uno estacione ahí, prioridades si tienen guía, malos modos...más allá de esos temas, vale la pena.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: The park was beautiful but very over rated. Tons of people are walking through stopping along single aisle paths and blocking individuals who may just want to pass. The guide I hired was great and always willing to top and allow us to get photos with our cell phone. If you have your own binoculars or tripod there would be no reason to have a guide. While you have a guide it's rush, rush to the next spot. Even if you want to hang around the monkey mafia along the boardwalk. After the guide is finished with his tour you can head out of the park or head back to the beaches or cafeteria. DO NOT EAT at the cafeteria if you can avoid it. The food was terrible and way overpriced. I don't know how you can screw up something as simple as ham and cheese sandwich but they did. It was covered in flour dust and both top and bottom giving a terrible flavor already drying out your mouth. The best part of the cafeteria was the ice cream and cold reverse osmosis water you could fill your water bottle with.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Maravilloso parque, de verdad FELICITO a todos quienes cuidan y conservan esta área de reserva natural. Para entrar se deben reservar las entradas en su pagina web con anticipación, tiene mucha demanda. Recomiendo el primer turno de la mañana a las 0700 hrs, así podrás disfrutar de el parque y sus playas, la salida es a mas tardar a las 1500 hrs. Recomiendo contratar un guía, en nuestro caso nos acompaño Alvin, ya que ellos pueden ver fauna  que a simple vista pasa desapercibida, además de conocer sus nombres y características. Debo destacar a nuestro guía, ALVIN conocedor del lugar y la biodiversidad que allí habita, muy apasionado y que realmente ama el lugar y su labor, nos enseño mucho respecto a como cuidar el medio ambiente.
Lleven agua en botella retornable no plástica, no se permite entrar ningún tipo de elemento que pueda contaminar el parque. Usen zapatillas cómodas, se debe caminar bastante y usen bloqueador solar. Las playas dentro del parque...  maravillosas.
Definitivamente volvería.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
The hype around this place is much bigger than the park itself. Having said that park is not uninteresting. You will better enjoy it if you are eco-geek or wildlife enthusiast. Although I saw more wildlife from my hotel room nearby than in the park. Beaches are fine but again, not much different than public ones just across the fence.
What you should know:
- Tickets are only sold through government website and should be booked in advance. Just showing up at the gate may bring you disappointment as you wont be able to get in. We tried booking one day ahead and found all tickets sold out. At the same time local guides manage to block big chunks of tickets and we had to buy a guided tour inside the park at $59 per person.
- If youre after the beach experience then tell that to the guide once you're inside. Otherwise you'll be stuck on a crowded trail staring through the guide's scope on various bugs and lizards for two hours.
- Dont bring with you any drinks in plastic bottles, food or sun sprays as those will be confiscated. Bring a metal water bottle and you can refill it at many spots inside the park.
- If you're a photographer and looking to shoot in the golden hour at dawn or at the sunset, tough luck. The park is not accessible in early mornings or at sunset time. Park staff is very active in clearing all beaches and trails starting at 3pm.

To summarize - guided tours for wildlife enthusiasts only. Beaches are fine but not much different to ones with free public access. Photographers - no golden hour for you. If you land in Liberia it may not be worth a drive but is a good place to visit for visitors arriving at San Jose.

I went to the beautiful Manuel Antonio National Park yesterday and absolutely loved it. I chose not to take a tour guide, and I’m glad I didn’t. The trails are beautifully maintained, and I saw many animals in their natural habitats, including sloths, agoutis, and monkeys.

I recommend going early to avoid the crowds on the trails. I arrived at 7 AM when the park opened and was able to explore in peace without having to compete with large groups while enjoying the experience.

review_text:
This national park needs to be on your list of places to go while in Costa Rica. Our grandkids loved watching the White faced monkeys and squirrel monkeys. We heard howler monkeys a few times but never got to see them. We did see a couple of sloths, but they can't be a challenge to spot in the trees.

We finished off the day at the beach, which was a nice way to cool off. I would recommend going to the 2nd beach you come to on the trail. A lot more people stopped at the first beach you come to. We found that the 2nd beach was a lot less crowded.

The people trying to stop you on the road and get you to park in their parking lot was interesting. Just keep going and don't even roll down your window. There's a pretty large paid parking lot right before the entrance to the park. You have to be out of the lot by 3pm, but if you start early, it will give you plenty of time.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
El Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio es uno de los lugares naturales más impresionantes que he visitado. La combinación de selva tropical, senderos bien cuidados y playas paradisíacas hace que la experiencia sea absolutamente única.

Durante el recorrido por los senderos es posible ver una gran variedad de fauna: monos, perezosos, iguanas, aves tropicales y muchos otros animales en su hábitat natural. Todo está muy bien organizado y el parque se mantiene limpio y protegido.

Además, dentro del parque hay playas de una belleza extraordinaria, con agua cristalina y arena clara, rodeadas de vegetación. Es el lugar perfecto para caminar, observar la naturaleza y luego terminar el día relajándose frente al mar.

Una visita imprescindible si viajas a Costa Rica. La naturaleza aquí es simplemente espectacular.

En mi caso fui en coche y no ameritaba guía, bastaba pagar la entrada al parque dejé el coche en el hotel y bajé en bus que cuesta menos de 1 euro.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Great park. Reservation online needed. Some tips:

Parking: There are “official” parking attendants who direct you where to park and ask you to pay exorbitant prices. They are basically scammers who prey on the foreign tourists. You can simply say you are going to a nearby cafe and park in the street and it’s free.

Trails and beaches: personally, I think you should come for after lunch (12-13:00). You will have until 3pm to enjoy the park and it feels slightly less crowded in the afternoon. Most trails close at 3pm so make sure you start them before then. The beaches also close at 3pm and you will be directed to leave the park, which closes at 4pm.

Canteen: horrible, overpriced. Bring your own water (plastic bottles not allowed). You cannot bring your own food so maybe eat before you enter.

Son matériel nous a permis de voir les animaux/insectes/reptiles très facilement et très nettement. Grâce à son téléphone fixe sur sa lunette, nous pouvions super bien voir et elle nous a ensuite envoyé les photos prises avec sa lunette. Qualité incroyable !!! Merci à Mile pour sa gentillesse, ses explications et ses superbes photos. Nous avons vu: paresseux, singes hurleurs, singes capucin,, serpent liane, iguane, araignée, insectes divers, oiseaux ++ etc…

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Visitar el Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio fue una experiencia inolvidable. La combinación de selva tropical, playas de arena blanca y agua cristalina es simplemente espectacular.

Mientras caminábamos por los senderos vimos monos, perezosos y mucha vida silvestre en su hábitat natural. Todo el parque está muy bien cuidado y organizado, lo que hace que el recorrido sea agradable y seguro.

Es uno de esos lugares donde realmente se siente la conexión con la naturaleza. Sin duda, una parada obligatoria si visitas Costa Rica.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
El parque destaca principalmente por su entorno natural y la belleza del trekking entre selva, playas y miradores. Los senderos permiten disfrutar del paisaje y de la experiencia de estar en contacto con la naturaleza, que sin dudas es el mayor atractivo del lugar.

Sin embargo, la infraestructura y el mantenimiento dejan bastante que desear. Durante la visita se notó un claro descuido general: los baños estaban sucios y mal mantenidos, la cartelería de los senderos es antigua, deteriorada y en algunos tramos insuficiente, lo que dificulta la orientación. También se percibe una falta de señalización clara y de puestos de hidratación a lo largo del recorrido, algo importante considerando el clima y la exigencia física.

Dentro del parque hay solo un restaurante, con opciones muy limitadas y sin comodidades básicas como aire acondicionado o espacios agradables para descansar. La zona gastronómica resulta desprolija y poco invitante, lo que no incentiva a permanecer más tiempo dentro del parque.

En conclusión, el Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio es recomendable para realizar un trekking y disfrutar del entorno natural, pero más allá de eso no invita a quedarse demasiado tiempo. Con una mejor gestión, mantenimiento e inversión en infraestructura básica, podría ofrecer una experiencia mucho más completa y acorde a la fama internacional que tiene.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: Der Park ist ganz nett, aber im Vergleich zu anderen Nationalparks in Costa Rica meiner Meinung nach eher nicht so interessant. Der Park ist absolut touristisch, vor dem Park wird man von vielen Leuten angesprochen die einem Touren, Parkplätze, Getränke verkaufen wollen das kann durchaus unangenehm sein. Im Park sind natürlich sehr viele Touristen man hat kaum das Gefühl in „richtiger Natur“ zu sein. Ohne Guide hätten wir außer Affen gar keine Tiere gesehen, vielleicht haben wir aber auch einen schlechten Tag erwischt. Daher würde ich allerdings eine Tour mit Guide schon empfehlen. Die Toiletten sind nicht sehr sauber. Es ist alles in allem okay gewesen, aber nicht herausragend gut.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Der Nationalpark Manuel Antonio in Costa Rica ist landschaftlich wirklich wunderschön. Die Natur ist beeindruckend, die Wege führen durch dichten Regenwald und man sieht tatsächlich viele Tiere – Affen, Faultiere, Echsen und verschiedene Vogelarten. Besonders schön ist auch der Strandabschnitt im Park, der mit seinem hellen Sand und dem türkisfarbenen Wasser.

Allerdings wird das positive Erlebnis leider stark durch die Menschenmassen getrübt. Obwohl man die Tickets vorab online buchen muss – was eigentlich den Eindruck vermittelt, dass die Besucheranzahl begrenzt wird – war der Park bei unserem Besuch extrem überfüllt. Es waren sehr große Gruppen mit Guides unterwegs, die häufig die Wege blockierten oder einem die Sicht versperrten. Auf den schmalen Pfaden kam man teilweise kaum durch. Insgesamt hatte man eher das Gefühl, durch eine überlaufene Touristenattraktion zu laufen als durch einen Nationalpark.

Ganz ehrlich: In Costa Rica sieht man auch außerhalb des Parks an vielen Orten Tiere in freier Wildbahn – und das kostenlos. Rein theoretisch könnte man sich das Geld für den Eintritt also sparen.

Ein Tipp zur Anreise: Am besten kommt man mit Uber, da die Parkplatzsituation vor Ort sehr schwierig ist. Außerdem wird man schon vor dem Parkeingang von vermeintlichen Guides angesprochen, die Touren anbieten. Diese führen zwar tatsächlich durch den Park, sind aber offenbar nicht wirklich geschult. Wir haben später mit anderen Besuchern gesprochen, die eine solche Tour gebucht hatten, und sie waren ziemlich enttäuscht.

Fazit: Wunderschöne Natur und tolle Tierbeobachtungen – aber leider viel zu überlaufen. Wer Ruhe sucht, wird hier vermutlich nicht glücklich.

Noch eine Info ! Man darf kein Essen und nur Getränke in wiederverwendbaren Flaschen mit rein nehmen. Selbst Kaugummi kauen ist verboten ( Schutz der Tiere, also Gute Sache )

name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text: Manuel Antonio is gorgeous, but why fight the parking, crowds, and cost when you can get the same thing for free? Hit the beaches along the pacific coast from Quepos to Ojuchal and you’ll find wildlife galore, pictures perfect beaches, great food, caves, tide pools, etc. make sure to be on the beach at dusk when the monkeys come out and of course at sunset.

query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
name: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio
review_text:
Na een heel gedoe met een half vertaalde website om de kaartjes te kopen sta je eerst een 30 minuten in de rij. Eerst om naar binnen te komen, dan de tassen controle en vervolgens voor de ticket controle. Eenmaal in het park is het ontzettend druk. Daar kun je wat vervelende opmerkingen verwachten van onfatsoenlijke gidsen als je perongeluk de kant op kijkt waar zij hun lens op gericht hebben. Iets in de hoek van "had je maar voor een gids moeten betalen".
De bewegwijzering in het park is onduidelijk en in slechte staat. Het is soms een beetje gokken of kijken waar je uitkomt.

Verder een mooi park maar veel te druk!
Ondanks dat we geen gids hadden, hebben we veel dieren gezien.

review_text: While this is a big tourist spot and worth a hike or walk through it has changed a lot since we visit the last time (13 years ago). It has become very touristy and you can hardly see any animals of you don't have a guide with you with a telescope or binoculars. query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica

review_text: Good national park, but, like the others, you need to book online, as it gets filled up. They only let in a limited number each day, with time slots. Pretty crowded, even so. query: Parque Nacional Manuel Antonio, Costa Rica
